text.title: earned-value-management Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for earned-value-management. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Use objective % complete only: 0/50/100 rule, milestone-based, or units-completed — never subjective opinion.
rule.rationale: Subjective % complete (team says "80% done") corrupts EV and cascades into false SPI/CPI/EAC values. One inflated EV reading misleads stakeholders for the rest of the project.

rule.statement: Color-code: SPI/CPI in [0.95, 1.05] = green; [0.85, 0.95) = yellow; below 0.85 = red.
rule.rationale: Consistent thresholds across all reporting periods allow stakeholders to compare status at a glance without reinterpreting scales each time.

rule.statement: Flag TCPI greater than 1.10 as likely unrecoverable; escalate to sponsor for re-baseline or scope reduction decision.
rule.rationale: TCPI greater than 1.10 means the remaining work must be completed 10% more efficiently than the project has averaged — statistically improbable without a structural intervention.

rule.statement: Track EVM at WBS level 3-4; not at the leaf task level and not at the project summary level only.
rule.rationale: Leaf-level tracking generates noise from individual task estimation error. Summary-level tracking hides which work packages are in trouble until the overall CPI collapses.

rule.statement: Treat re-baselining as a P1 event; log it, require CCB approval, never automate.
rule.rationale: Silent re-baselining is the most common way teams game EVM. A team with CPI 0.70 can re-baseline to show CPI 1.00 overnight. Require an explicit decision record for every re-baseline.

rule.statement: Enforce single base currency; agents must not silently mix USD/EUR/GBP in AC.
rule.rationale: Currency mixing produces nonsense CPI values. Multi-currency programs need FX-hedged EAC with explicit conversion date and rate.
